title: The Emperor of All Maladies
excerpt: A sweeping biography of cancer — from its first documented appearances to the cutting-edge molecular therapies of the 21st century — told through the doctors, scientists, and patients who fought it.
keyIdeas: Cancer is not one disease but hundreds, each with unique genetic drivers
The war on cancer has been fought in incremental advances, not a single breakthrough
Cancer's origin lies in the very mechanisms that sustain life (cell division)
The history of cancer treatment mirrors the history of modern medicine
keyTakeaways: Cancer is a disease of the genome — caused by accumulated mutations in DNA
Chemotherapy, radiation, and surgery have saved millions but with devastating side effects
Targeted therapies (Gleevec, Herceptin) represent a paradigm shift in treatment
Prevention and early detection remain the most powerful weapons against cancer
whoShouldRead: Anyone affected by cancer (patients, families, caregivers)
Readers interested in the history of science and medicine
Fans of narrative nonfiction and medical writing
whoShouldSkip: Those looking for treatment advice or medical guidance
Readers who are easily distressed by detailed medical descriptions
People seeking a purely scientific textbook on oncology
